methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $529M - $6.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring

data.narrative:
Freenome is a public-stage biotech and health company founded by Gabriel Otte. The peer universe consists of 3 public comparables (Recursion Pharmaceuticals, Tempus AI, and Alto Neuroscience) operating in the same sector and stage, with valuations ranging from $529M to $6.0B.

Peer-median analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. The median valuation across the 3 same-sector, same-stage peers is $1.8B, with Recursion Pharmaceuticals at $1.8B, Alto Neuroscience at $529M, and Tempus AI at $6.0B anchoring the distribution. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied because Freenome's revenue is not on file; the sector median P/S of 7.4x across the 3 public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) cannot be reliably applied without this denominator.

The triangulated range supporting Freenome's valuation is $1.5B to $2.1B, with a median of $1.8B. The current valuation of $2.6B sits above this range, suggesting a premium relative to public peers. Key uncertainties include the lack of revenue visibility for direct multiple application and the wide dispersion within the peer set ($529M to $6.0B), which reflects heterogeneity in biotech valuations. A single-method analysis limits conviction; additional revenue or margin data would enable P/S or EV/EBITDA cross-checks.
